changes_made: Reviewed and confirmed the naming convention for 'k8s_networkpolicy_allow_by_exception' to ensure it aligns with the 'deny by default, allow by exception' principle.
review_notes: The functions accurately map to the NIST SC-7(5) control by implementing default deny policies for ingress and egress traffic in Kubernetes using NetworkPolicies. The naming is mostly consistent with the established pattern, but 'k8s_networkpolicy_allow_by_exception' needed a review to confirm its clarity and applicability. It is important to ensure that this function clearly indicates that exceptions are explicitly allowed, which aligns with the control's requirements.
title: Boundary Protection - Boundary protection | deny by default — allow by exception deny network communic
step1_reasoning: The control SC-7(5) focuses on boundary protection by enforcing a 'deny by default, allow by exception' policy for network communications. In Kubernetes, this can be implemented using NetworkPolicies to ensure that ingress and egress traffic is denied by default, and only explicitly allowed traffic is permitted. This aligns with the control's requirement to restrict network communication by default and allow only specified exceptions.
